MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication.

APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McGrew was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability against defendant. Notwithstanding the defects of the written discovery that defendant propounded, plaintiffs' responses to special interrogatories and requests for production and the deposition testimony of Mr. Larson and Mr. Graham also preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Larson's deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. Graham's deposition testimony, defendant's General Order No. 129 responses and the deposition testimony of Mr. Merrill would create a triable issue whether Mr. McGrew was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Paco joint compound) attributable to defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Iva McGrew, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT PACE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance. Tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Pace, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Clarence McGrew, Jr. was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories, specifically interrogatory no. 3, preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs properly identified their witnesses, as requested, and defendant failed to demonstrate that those persons do not have knowledge supporting plaintiffs' claims. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an element of plaintiffs' claims. Even if admissible, Mr. Schuler's declaration is insufficient. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
